Now speaking

The written briefing

World

  • Middle East — update. The US is moving to blockade Iran's ports as the standoff over the Strait of Hormuz drags on; Washington insists Iran doesn't control the strait while Tehran claims it does. Oil markets are on edge. RFE/RL · Al Jazeera
  • World Cup. Spain beat France 2–0 (Oyarzabal penalty, Porro) to reach Sunday's final; England v Argentina settle the other semifinal tonight (~midnight Athens). CNN · ESPN
  • Science: astronomers published the largest gravitational-wave catalogue ever — 161 new black-hole collisions, bringing the total to 390 detections. ScienceDaily

AI & Tech

  • Google Gemini Enterprise. At Google Cloud Next '26, Google unveiled an expanded portfolio to build, orchestrate and govern fleets of AI agents that plug into company data under IT control. buildfastwithai
  • Claude reflection dashboard. Anthropic shipped a beta dashboard that visualises how you use Claude, lets you set quiet hours, and nudges more intentional use (web + desktop, Memory on). Anthropic
  • Rumor. Anthropic is reportedly in talks with Samsung to build a custom AI chip tuned to Claude — unconfirmed. buildfastwithai

Weather — Athens

  • Hot and dry. Low 20s in the morning, near 33°C in the afternoon, mid-20s by evening. Strong northerly meltemi keeps it breezy but raises fire risk; no rain. Sunset ~20:45. easeWeather · Keep Talking Greece

Personal & Communication

  • Calendar connector unavailable — check it manually.
  • 2 inbox items worth attention: 1 payment/account action needed, and 1 subscription auto-renewing tomorrow. Everything else is receipts, sale emails and routine notifications.

Gaming & Deals

  • Epic free games — last day. Nova Lands and Tattoo Tycoon roll over tomorrow (Jul 16); a surprise freebie, NoRush! – Tower Edition, is claimable through Jul 20. PC Gamer
  • New on Steam today: co-op Lovecraftian horror The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu. GameGrin

Learning

  • Word of the day — flash: in climbing, to complete a route clean on your first attempt when you already have the beta. "He flashed the boulder problem after a single look."
  • Productivity — the two-minute rule: if a task will take under two minutes, do it now rather than filing it away — the filing usually costs more than the doing.
